question_text: A ball is dropped from a height of 10 metres. As it falls, gravitational potential energy is converted into kinetic energy. Assuming no energy is lost to air resistance, what can you say about the total mechanical energy of the ball throughout its fall?

options.label: A
options.text: The total mechanical energy decreases because kinetic energy is being used up

options.label: B
options.text: The total mechanical energy stays the same because energy is transferred between stores, not created or destroyed

options.label: C
options.text: The total mechanical energy increases as the ball falls and gains speed

options.label: D
options.text: The total mechanical energy becomes zero when the ball reaches the ground

explanation: Energy cannot be created or destroyed—it only transfers between different stores. As the ball falls, gravitational potential energy decreases while kinetic energy increases by exactly the same amount, keeping the total mechanical energy constant.
